# ipmi Provider

Manage BMC/iDRAC hardware via IPMI 2.0 (LAN+) by wrapping the ipmitool CLI.

Resources can override any of these fields per-host for multi-BMC fleets.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `cipher_suite` (Number) RMCP+ cipher suite ID. **Required** — no safe default. Common values:
  - `3`: RAKP-HMAC-SHA1 + AES-CBC-128. Legacy Dell 11G, iDRAC6.
  - `17`: RAKP-HMAC-SHA256 + AES-CBC-128. iDRAC7+, SuperMicro X10+, AsRock Rack, modern hardware.
  - `0`: no auth, no integrity. Requires `allow_unauthenticated = true`.

Run `make detect-cipher HOST=... USER=...` against a BMC to probe (warning: each failed probe is one failed auth attempt; iDRAC default lockout = 3 strikes).

### Optional

- `allow_unauthenticated` (Boolean) Opt-in confirmation that cipher_suite = 0 (no RMCP+ authentication or integrity) is intentional. Setting cipher_suite = 0 without this flag is a Configure-time error. Never set this for production.
- `host` (String) Default BMC IP or hostname. Resources may override.
- `interface` (String) ipmitool interface: lanplus (default), lan, or open.
- `password` (String, Sensitive) Default IPMI password.
- `port` (Number) Default IPMI UDP port. Defaults to 623.
- `timeout_seconds` (Number) Per-call timeout for ipmitool subprocess. Defaults to 60s — older BMCs are slow on `sdr list` (full SDR iteration).
- `username` (String) Default IPMI username.
